title: rubus vermontanus var. ortivus
intro: Rubus vermontanus var. ortivus, also known as the Orton's black raspberry, is a variety of black raspberry native to the eastern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, wounds, and fever, due to its ability to promote healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Rubus vermontanus var. ortivus can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, allowing for topical and internal use of its medicinal properties.
